scope.summary: Implement a seam mutation operation (edit_seam MCP tool and CRDT delta) that updates gather_ratio and SeamEndpoint::range on an existing SeamSpec in a draft GarmentSpec without re-authoring the entire garment
acceptance_criteria: The edit_seam MCP tool accepts (garment_id, seam_id, patch: { gather_ratio?, range_from?, range_to? }) and applies the patch to the draft SeamSpec in tailor_garments, emitting a TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded (seam-mutation tombstone + new entry) event
The GATHER_RATIO_RANGE blocking check fires at patch time and rejects out-of-range values with a structured error including field_path
The CRDT conflict rule for concurrent seam edits is last-writer-wins per seam_id (seam_id is stable within a garment version, per MT-135 CRDT discipline)
The edited gather_ratio takes effect in the next sandbox simulation run without requiring full garment re-draft
The ModelManual entry for edit_seam lists the legal patch fields, error taxonomy (InvalidSpec, GATHER_RATIO_RANGE), and self-correction pattern (SimulationReceipt -> suggested patch -> rerun)
scope.risk_if_missed: Gather ratio tuning is the primary iterative control for ruffles, pleats, and ease. In MD a designer adjusts sewing line length numerically after observing the draped result, re-sims, and repeats. Without edit_seam a model must re-author the entire GarmentSpec from scratch to change a single gather value — destroying the self-correction loop (MT-268) and making iterative garment refinement 10x more expensive. This blocks the arousal-engineering equivalent of 'adjust gather until the ruffle looks right'.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
